title: resolve — deterministic dependency ordering
prompt: Write a Python function with this exact signature:
deps is a dict mapping each task name (a string) to a list of prerequisite task
names that must be completed before it. Simulate completion as follows:
- Repeatedly look at all tasks that are not yet completed and whose
- Among those, complete the alphabetically smallest one and append its name to
- Stop when no not-yet-completed task has all its prerequisites completed.
Return the list of completed task names in completion order. If a cycle (or a
missing prerequisite) leaves some tasks never ready, they are simply never
completed and do not appear in the result.
Respond with a SINGLE Python code block (```python ... ```) defining resolve and
nothing else. Include no explanation outside the code block.
difficulty_rationale: Topological ordering with two non-obvious requirements the hidden asserts enforce: a deterministic alphabetical tiebreak among simultaneously-ready tasks (re-evaluated each step, not a one-shot sort), and graceful partial output on cycles (returns [] rather than looping or erroring). Reference-implementation-verified. Expected to reward higher tiers that handle the cycle and tiebreak correctly.
